integrity.caveat: SEED_SPECS in schemas/seed_data.py informed synthetic case authoring; final labels in data/labels/labels.json were human-confirmed separately. Agent prompts and planner logic do not read labels at runtime.

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Prior Authorization Department,

We are submitting a prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for our patient Jordan Blake (age 52) with rheumatoid arthritis.

Clinical Summary:
- Diagnosis: Rheumatoid arthritis (ICD-10 M06.9), RF and anti-CCP positive
- Disease duration: 14 months
- Failed conventional DMARDs: 2 documented trials
- Current DAS28 score: 4.8 (moderate-to-high disease activity)
- Methotrexate trial: 16 weeks at stable dose

This request meets all payer policy criteria for adalimumab coverage. Please process this authorization request at your earliest convenience.

Thank you,
Rheumatology Clinic
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Submission - Case case-001 - Adalimumab for Jordan Blake

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Rheumatology Team,

We are processing the prior-authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Jamie Ortiz (Case ID: case-007).

The patient meets most payer criteria:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9, RF/anti-CCP positive) ✓
- Disease duration: 10 months (≥6 months required) ✓
- Failed 2 conventional DMARDs ✓
- Methotrexate trial: 14 weeks (≥12 weeks required) ✓

However, the payer requires a DAS28 score of at least 3.2, which is missing from the clinical documentation.

Please provide:
- Current DAS28 score

Once received, we can complete the prior-authorization submission.

Thank you,
Prior-Auth Triage Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior-Auth case-007: Missing DAS28 Score for Adalimumab Request

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Rheumatology Team,

Prior authorization case-011 for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Alex Mercer (age 42) requires additional documentation before submission.

MISSING REQUIREMENT:
- Confirmed rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis with appropriate ICD-10 code (M05 or M06). Current documentation states 'inflammatory arthritis suspected' which does not meet payer requirements for confirmed diagnosis.

CRITERIA ALREADY MET:
✓ Disease duration: 7 months (≥6 months required)
✓ Failed DMARDs: 2 (≥2 required)
✓ DAS28 score: 3.5 (≥3.2 required)
✓ Methotrexate trial: 12 weeks (≥12 weeks required)

ACTION NEEDED:
Please provide formal diagnostic confirmation of rheumatoid arthritis with appropriate ICD-10 coding (M05 or M06) to proceed with prior authorization submission.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth case-011: Additional Documentation Needed for Alex Mercer Humira Request

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Rheumatology Team,

This is a notification regarding prior-authorization case-012 for patient Sam Rivera (age 57) requesting adalimumab (Humira) for rheumatoid arthritis.

DENIAL RISK: The current clinical documentation does not meet payer policy requirements.

Reason: The patient's DAS28 score of 2.4 falls below the required threshold of 3.2 for biologic therapy coverage. A DAS28 of 2.4 indicates low disease activity, which does not support medical necessity for adalimumab per payer criteria.

All other criteria are met:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9)
- Disease duration: 10 months (≥6 months required)
- Failed 2 conventional DMARDs (≥2 required)
- Methotrexate trial: 14 weeks (≥12 weeks required)

Recommendation: If there are clinical factors justifying biologic therapy despite low disease activity (e.g., recent flare, erosive disease progression, extracurricular manifestations), please provide additional documentation for peer-to-peer review or appeal. Otherwise, this authorization is likely to be denied.

Please contact the prior-auth team if you have questions.

Best regards,
Prior-Auth Triage Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior-Auth Denial Risk: case-012 - Sam Rivera - Adalimumab (Humira)

case_results.drafted_email:
Case ID: case-016
Patient: Finley Shaw, age 45
Drug: Adalimumab (Humira)
Condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis

DECISION: DENY - Does not meet payer clinical criteria

RATIONALE:
The patient's current DAS28 score is 3.0, which is below the payer's required threshold of 3.2 for adalimumab coverage. While all other criteria are met (confirmed RA diagnosis, disease duration 8 months, 2 failed DMARDs, 12-week methotrexate trial), the DAS28 score requirement is not satisfied.

RECOMMENDATION:
Consider re-assessment of disease activity or optimization of current DMARD therapy. A prior authorization request may be resubmitted if the patient's DAS28 score increases to ≥3.2 on repeat measurement.

Please contact the prior authorization team if you have questions or wish to discuss this case.
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth DENIED - Case-016: Adalimumab for Finley Shaw - DAS28 Below Threshold

Clinical Information:
- Diabetes duration: 5 years
- Most recent A1C: 8.2%
- Metformin trial: 6 months at maximum tolerated dose
- BMI: 33.1

This request meets all payer policy requirements:
✓ A1C ≥ 7.0% (patient: 8.2%)
✓ Metformin trial ≥ 3 months (patient: 6 months)
✓ BMI documented (33.1)

Please approve coverage for semaglutide (Ozempic) for glycemic control.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
Endocrinology Prior Authorization Team

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient Aspen Cole (Case ID: case-030) does not meet payer coverage criteria.

Reason for denial:
- Patient's most recent A1C is 6.9%, which is below the required threshold of at least 7.0% per payer policy.

Documented clinical information:
- A1C: 6.9%
- Metformin trial: 6 months (meets requirement)
- BMI: 28.0 (documented)
- Diabetes duration: 4 years

The patient meets all other criteria but does not satisfy the minimum A1C requirement for coverage. Please consider:
1. Reassessing glycemic control targets and continuing current therapy
2. Resubmitting if A1C rises to ≥7.0% on future testing
3. Appealing with clinical justification if there are compelling reasons for therapy despite A1C <7.0%

Please contact our office with any questions.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denial - Semaglutide (Ozempic) for Aspen Cole - Case-030

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Prior Authorization Department,

We are submitting a prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) for our patient Luna Pierce (age 45) for the treatment of chronic migraine.

Clinical Summary:
- Diagnosis: Chronic migraine (ICD-10: G43.7)
- Migraine days per month: 20
- Failed triptan trials: 3
- Failed preventive medication trials: 2

This request meets all payer coverage criteria:
✓ Chronic migraine diagnosis confirmed
✓ At least 15 migraine days per month (patient has 20)
✓ Documented failure of at least 2 triptans (patient failed 3)
✓ Failure of at least 1 preventive migraine medication (patient failed 2)

Please process this prior authorization request. Should you require additional clinical documentation, please contact our office.

Thank you,
Neurology Department

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Neurology Team,

We are processing the prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) for patient Aria Quinn (age 46, Case ID: case-043).

The payer requires documentation of failure of at least 1 preventive migraine medication. The current clinical note documents:
- 21 migraine days per month (meets chronic migraine criteria)
- Failure of 2 triptans (meets requirement)

However, we need additional information:
- Has the patient failed at least one preventive migraine medication (e.g., beta-blockers like propranolol, anticonvulsants like topiramate or valproate, antidepressants like amitriptyline)?
- Please document the medication name(s), duration of trial, and reason for discontinuation or failure.

Please provide this information so we can complete the prior authorization review.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior-Auth case-043: Additional Information Needed for Aimovig (Aria Quinn)

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) for patient Faye Knox (Case ID: case-044) does not meet payer medical necessity criteria.

Reason for denial:
The patient reports 8 migraine days per month, which does not meet the payer's required threshold of at least 15 migraine days per month for chronic migraine coverage.

While the patient has:
- Confirmed chronic migraine diagnosis (G43.7)
- Failed 2 triptan trials (meets requirement)
- Failed 1 preventive medication trial (meets requirement)

The migraine frequency criterion is not satisfied.

Next steps:
- If the patient's migraine frequency has increased or was inaccurately documented, please provide updated clinical documentation showing at least 15 migraine days per month
- Consider resubmission if clinical status changes to meet payer criteria
- Discuss alternative treatment options with the patient

Please contact our office if you have questions or wish to appeal this determination.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denial - Case case-044 - Faye Knox - Erenumab (Aimovig)

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) for patient Jade Poole (age 55, Case ID: case-048) does not meet payer policy requirements and is recommended for denial.

Reason for denial:
While the patient meets several criteria (chronic migraine diagnosis, 18 migraine days/month, and failure of 2 triptans), the payer policy requires documented failure of at least 1 preventive migraine medication. The current clinical documentation shows 0 failed preventive medication trials.

To resubmit:
Please initiate and document a trial of at least one preventive migraine medication (e.g., beta-blocker, antiepileptic, or tricyclic antidepressant) and document therapeutic failure before resubmitting the prior authorization request.

If you believe preventive medications have been tried but were not captured in the documentation, please provide additional clinical records.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Aimovig for Jade Poole (Case-048)
